turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#f7f1e1" />

  <!-- shadow under cup -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="410" rx="120" ry="18" fill="#000" opacity="0.08" />

  <!-- saucer -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="380" rx="150" ry="28" fill="#efefef" stroke="#e0e0e0" stroke-width="2" />

  <!-- cup body -->
  <rect x="156" y="300" width="200" height="120" rx="20" ry="20" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#d0d0d0" stroke-width="4" />

  <!-- cup rim outline -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="320" rx="102" ry="40" fill="none" stroke="#d0d0d0" stroke-width="4" />

  <!-- coffee surface -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="320" rx="82" ry="34" fill="#3b2b22" />

  <!-- handle (outer) -->
  <path d="M356 330 a40 40 0 1 0 0 80" stroke="#d0d0d0" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <!-- handle (inner white) -->
  <path d="M356 335 a30 30 0 1 0 0 70" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="10" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />

  <!-- steam -->
  <path d="M220 250 C216 230, 228 210, 234 190" stroke="#bdbdbd" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <path d="M256 245 C252 225, 266 205, 270 185" stroke="#bdbdbd" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <path d="M292 248 C288 228, 304 208, 308 188" stroke="#bdbdbd" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />

  <!-- small drop and ripple on coffee to suggest strong flavor -->
  <circle cx="220" cy="322" r="6" fill="#2f1f18" opacity="0.9" />
  <ellipse cx="220" cy="330" rx="18" ry="6" fill="#000" opacity="0.04" />

  <!-- face showing dislike (grimace) -->
  <g transform="translate(360,160)">
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="36" fill="#ffd9b3" stroke="#e0b88a" stroke-width="3" />
    <!-- eyes: squinting -->
    <path d="M-16 -6 q8 -10 16 0" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <path d="M-8 -6 q8 -10 16 0" transform="translate(-4,0)" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- frowning mouth -->
    <path d="M-18 12 q18 18 36 0" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- small wrinkle lines between eyes for displeasure -->
    <path d="M-6 -14 q6 6 12 0" stroke="#e09973" stroke-width="3" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>

  <!-- subtle bitter indicator: a tiny jagged line near cup to suggest strong taste -->
  <path d="M300 340 l8 -6 l-6 -6 l10 -4" stroke="#6b3f2e" stroke-width="3" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" opacity="0.9" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

/>\\n</svg>\"\n}","refusal":null,"reasoning":"**Representing \"bitter\" visually**\n\nI'm thinking about how to draw \"bitter\" without using any text. Maybe I could depict a disgusted facial expression or a coffee cup since bitter is often linked to coffee. I wonder if I should show dark coffee with steam? A lemon seems too sour, though. Another idea is drawing a person with crossed arms, looking cold and frowning. Maybe a coffee cup with a frowning face could work, along with a taste symbol. A skull-and-crossbones might be a bold option too!**Illustrating \"bitter\" visually**\n\nI’m trying to draw “bitter” without implying poison in a straightforward way. A steaming cup of black coffee seems like a good option, maybe with a taste bud icon. I can't include letters or numbers, but I can draw a simple face tasting the coffee with a scrunched mouth above the cup. Alternatively, I could show a stylized tongue sticking out. A sour face might work too, but I want to focus on bitterness, so I'll brainstorm further.**Designing a \"bitter\" representation**\n\nI'm leaning towards using a coffee cup to evoke the idea of bitterness. {
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#f7f1e1" />

  <!-- shadow under cup -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="410" rx="120" ry="18" fill="#000" opacity="0.08" />

  <!-- saucer -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="380" rx="150" ry="28" fill="#efefef" stroke="#e0e0e0" stroke-width="2" />

  <!-- cup body -->
  <rect x="156" y="300" width="200" height="120" rx="20" ry="20" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#d0d0d0" stroke-width="4" />

  <!-- cup rim outline -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="320" rx="102" ry="40" fill="none" stroke="#d0d0d0" stroke-width="4" />

  <!-- coffee surface -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="320" rx="82" ry="34" fill="#3b2b22" />

  <!-- handle (outer) -->
  <path d="M356 330 a40 40 0 1 0 0 80" stroke="#d0d0d0" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <!-- handle (inner white) -->
  <path d="M356 335 a30 30 0 1 0 0 70" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="10" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />

  <!-- steam -->
  <path d="M220 250 C216 230, 228 210, 234 190" stroke="#bdbdbd" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <path d="M256 245 C252 225, 266 205, 270 185" stroke="#bdbdbd" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  <path d="M292 248 C288 228, 304 208, 308 188" stroke="#bdbdbd" stroke-width="6" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />

  <!-- small drop and ripple on coffee to suggest strong flavor -->
  <circle cx="220" cy="322" r="6" fill="#2f1f18" opacity="0.9" />
  <ellipse cx="220" cy="330" rx="18" ry="6" fill="#000" opacity="0.04" />

  <!-- face showing dislike (grimace) -->
  <g transform="translate(360,160)">
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="36" fill="#ffd9b3" stroke="#e0b88a" stroke-width="3" />
    <!-- eyes: squinting -->
    <path d="M-16 -6 q8 -10 16 0" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <path d="M-8 -6 q8 -10 16 0" transform="translate(-4,0)" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- frowning mouth -->
    <path d="M-18 12 q18 18 36 0" stroke="#2b2b2b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <!-- small wrinkle lines between eyes for displeasure -->
    <path d="M-6 -14 q6 6 12 0" stroke="#e09973" stroke-width="3" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>

  <!-- subtle bitter indicator: a tiny jagged line near cup to suggest strong taste -->
  <path d="M300 340 l8 -6 l-6 -6 l10 -4" stroke="#6b3f2e" stroke-width="3" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" opacity="0.9" />
</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Representing "bitter" visually**

I'm thinking about how to draw "bitter" without using any text. Maybe I could depict a disgusted facial expression or a coffee cup since bitter is often linked to coffee. I wonder if I should show dark coffee with steam? A lemon seems too sour, though. Another idea is drawing a person with crossed arms, looking cold and frowning. Maybe a coffee cup with a frowning face could work, along with a taste symbol. A skull-and-crossbones might be a bold option too!**Illustrating "bitter" visually**

I’m trying to draw “bitter” without implying poison in a straightforward way. A steaming cup of black coffee seems like a good option, maybe with a taste bud icon. I can't include letters or numbers, but I can draw a simple face tasting the coffee with a scrunched mouth above the cup. Alternatively, I could show a stylized tongue sticking out. A sour face might work too, but I want to focus on bitterness, so I'll brainstorm further.**Designing a "bitter" representation**

I'm leaning towards using a coffee cup to evoke the idea of bitterness. I'll keep it simple with light beige for the background and a white cup with dark brown coffee. Steam lines and a small, wavy face near the mouth could indicate a grimace, but I can't use text. Instead, I’ll draw a stylized head with scrunched eyes and a frown sipping from the cup. Next to the cup, I might place a small triangular taste symbol showing puckered lips. Keeping the SVG simple is key!**Crafting a coffee cup SVG**

I'm starting with the base for the coffee cup SVG. The shadow will be an ellipse, while the cup itself can be a rounded rectangle. I’ll make the handle with a circular arc and the coffee as a dark brown ellipse inside. For steam, I'll draw three wavy paths above the cup. The face will be a simple circle with squinting eyes and a downward mouth but no text. I'll keep the colors minimal: a white cup, dark brown coffee, and a beige background. Now, let's set coordinates to create it!**Building the SVG structure**

I’m setting up the SVG elements for my design. The background will be a light beige rectangle, and I'll add an ellipse for the shadow under the cup. The cup itself will be a white rectangle with rounded corners, and there'll be a saucer underneath as an ellipse. For the coffee, I'll use a dark brown ellipse. The handle will be a thick stroked arc, and three paths will represent steam above the coffee. A simple face with squinting eyes and a frown will add a touch of character. Now to finalize the SVG string with proper formatting!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
